description: Public reaction to Edward Snowden's 2013 leaks regarding the NSA's PRISM surveillance program. The controversy centered on the tension between national security and digital privacy rights.

key_events.description: The Guardian publishes the first reports on the PRISM program.
Government officials testify to Congress defending the legality of the programs.
Mainstream media pivot to focusing on the 'betrayal' aspect of the leaks.
Public polling stabilizes as the initial shock transitions into partisan political debate.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling how high initial shock/sympathy for a whistleblower shifts toward institutional stability over time.
